# Add semantic-anchor lint for agent refs

## Context
Epic #11558 requires a correctness substrate before semantic-anchor migration PRs can safely proceed. Discussion #11557 v5 selected Option C globally and made lint/CI one of the risk-mitigation pillars replacing phased execution.

## The Problem
Without automated linting, new `§N` positional references can re-enter live skill or instruction substrate after the migration. Cross-family review can catch some cases, but the policy needs a mechanical merge-gate to prevent regression.

## The Architectural Reality
The existing lint surface is `ai/scripts/lint-agents.mjs` and the CI check `lint-skill-manifest`. Discussion #11557's cascade-correctness substrate specifically calls for extending this lint path to flag new positional refs in skill files. This ticket modifies an existing build/lint script; it does not introduce a new `.mjs` file.

## The Fix
Extend the agent lint path to detect forbidden live-substrate positional references and validate semantic-anchor usage according to the ADR from #11559.

Expected scope:
- `ai/scripts/lint-agents.mjs`
- tests or fixtures for allowed historical/archaeology exceptions and disallowed live `§N` refs
- CI evidence from `lint-skill-manifest`

## Contract Ledger Matrix

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Agent lint | New ADR from #11559 + Epic #11558 | Fail when new live skill refs use positional `§N` instead of semantic anchors | Cross-family review while lint evolves | ADR + lint error text | Unit/fixture coverage and CI run |
| Historical exceptions | New ADR from #11559 | Allow explicitly marked archaeology refs | Manual review if classification ambiguous | ADR | Fixture coverage |

## Discussion Criteria Mapping

- Cascade-correctness substrate -> lint + CI gate.
- Step 2.5 path determinism -> lint verifies semantic identity where practical.
- Active/archive boundary -> lint supports or documents exception handling.
- Migration blast-radius partial -> lint gives merge-gate confidence during partitioned PRs.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Lint fails for new live `.agents/skills/**` references that use raw positional `§N` anchors.
- [ ] Lint allows explicitly classified historical/archaeology references according to the ADR policy.
- [ ] Tests/fixtures cover failing and allowed cases.
- [ ] CI command for `lint-skill-manifest` exercises the new guard.
- [ ] Lint error text points authors toward semantic-anchor references and the ADR.

## Out of Scope

- Migrating all existing references.
- Defining the ADR policy itself.
- Enforcing runtime framework behavior.

## Avoided Traps / Gold Standards Rejected

- Review-only enforcement is rejected because it cannot prevent recurrence across every future session and PR.
- Over-broad banning of every historical `§N` occurrence is rejected because archaeology refs may intentionally point to historical states.

## Related

- Parent Epic: #11558
- Policy ADR ticket: #11559
- Discussion: #11557

- 2026-05-18T07:20:13Z @tobiu referenced in commit `5f4c575` - "feat(ci): PR-diff-scoped semantic-anchor lint for agent skills (#11560) (#11572)

Adds `ai/scripts/lint-agents.mjs` and wires it into the existing
`skill-manifest-lint` workflow as a second step. The lint flags NEW live
positional `§N` references in `.agents/skills/**` markdown per ADR 0011
(Substrate Numbering Convention, #11559).

Scope is intentionally diff-scoped: pre-existing references (264+ across
`.agents/skills/` at introduction time) remain untouched; they migrate
under sibling Epic #11558 sub-tickets (#11561 AGENTS/ATLAS, #11562 skills,
#11564 ADRs/docs). This script is the regression gate preventing new
positional refs from re-entering live substrate after migration.

Historical / archaeology / errata references are allowed via same-line
classification per ADR 0011 §2.3 (e.g. `historical: §21`,
`ADR 0007 recorded the old §21 disposition`).

Coverage: 26 unit tests passing — `parseArgs`, `isInScope`,
`isHistoricalContext`, `POSITIONAL_REF_PATTERN`, `findViolationsInLine`,
`parseAddedLines`, `lintDiff`, CLI happy-path, AC5 error-text pointer.

Co-authored-by: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>"
- 2026-05-18T07:20:13Z @tobiu closed this issue
- 2026-05-18T18:04:54Z @neo-opus-4-7 cross-referenced by PR #11586
- 2026-05-18T18:11:58Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `8ce9077` - "fix(lint): invert lint-agents.mjs to block <a id> tags (#11584)

Inverts prior #11560 enforcement. Operator-corrected substrate per Discussion
#11577 graduation (DC_kwDODSospM4BAt-P) permits §<ref> form (positional or
semantic) and treats HTML <a id> / <a name> scaffolding as damage in Map
substrate, .agents/skills/ skill substrate, and learn/agentos/ Agent OS
substrate.

Changes:
- ANCHOR_TAG_PATTERN matches <a id="..."> and <a name="..."> with attribute-
  boundary guard (\s(?:id|name)\s*=) — rejects <a href> regular hyperlinks
  and <abbr> / <article> false positives.
- Scope expanded from .agents/skills/ to Map substrate (AGENTS.md /
  AGENTS_STARTUP.md / .agents/ANTIGRAVITY_RULES.md) + .agents/skills/ +
  learn/agentos/. learn/guides/ intentionally excluded (user-facing docs
  carry legitimate HTML anchor examples).
- Adds 'rendered-consumer' exemption marker for narrow operator-approved
  exceptions (preserves historical / archaeology / errata markers).
- Updates JSDoc + CLI help + error messaging to cite Discussion #11577
  graduation and the §<ref> text-only primitive.
- Playwright spec rewritten: 45 tests covering scope expansion, §<ref>
  preservation, exemption-marker handling, false-positive avoidance.

Empirical damage anchor: PR #11571 (3d4c01f7) — 36 <a id> tags + 46
markdown-link refs + 4,358 bytes across AGENTS.md / AGENTS_STARTUP.md /
AGENTS_ATLAS.md."
